description: A constructor call with more arguments than declared fields raises `ol-too-many-inputs` (issue #329, `spec/data-structures.md:284`): the parenthesized `(point 3 4 5)` over-supplies the 2-field `point`. The bare `point 3 4 5` form instead groups by arity and leaves a stray token (`ol-bad-token`), identical to how a fixed-arity user procedure reads its arguments — so over-supply is expressed with the same parenthesized form the language uses everywhere else.
